source.name: IBM Carbon Design System
source.license_notes: Use as a source link and summarized guidance; do not copy full documentation verbatim.

layout_rules: Place table actions and filters close to the table they affect.
Keep primary actions visually distinct from secondary batch actions.
Use persistent column labels and predictable row alignment for scannability.
accessibility_notes: Keyboard navigation and visible focus states are required for dense data surfaces.
Status, sorting, and selection should not rely on color alone.
implementation_hints: Use when the user asks for serious B2B, admin, analytics, or enterprise UI.
Favor restraint over decorative gradients on data-heavy pages.
Expose empty/error/loading states in code, not only the happy path.

quality.notes: Seed record pointing to an official design system; expand with component-specific notes during detailed ingestion.

retrieval.ai_summary: Use Carbon-style patterns for dense enterprise data tables: neutral palette, strong alignment, toolbar/filter locality, accessible row states, and explicit empty/loading/error handling.
